agent1_snippets.title: Obesity as a Chronic Disease: Epidemiology and Pathophysiologic Insights -- Pathophysiology -- Traditional set point theory
agent1_snippets.content: The set point theory proposes that body weight is regulated around a predetermined "set point" through homeostatic mechanisms that resist deviations from this weight. Supporting evidence includes animal studies demonstrating weight return to baseline levels after forced feeding or caloric restriction, and twin studies showing similar body weights among identical twins raised in different environments. agent1_snippets.title: Twin Births (Archived) -- Pearls and Other Issues
agent1_snippets.content: Prevention Preconception Counseling: Encouraging preconception counseling for individuals contemplating pregnancy, specifically a pregnancy achieved by assisted reproductive technologies, to discuss the incidence of twin pregnancy and the resulting potential risks, to optimize maternal health, and to address any pre-existing medical conditions. Early Prenatal Care: Timely initiation of prenatal care allows for the early detection of complications and the implementation of preventive measures. Lifestyle Modifications: Emphasizing the importance of a healthy lifestyle, including proper nutrition, adequate rest, and avoidance of harmful substances, to minimize the risk of complications.

agent1_snippets.title: Twin Births (Archived) -- Evaluation
agent1_snippets.content: Regarding fetal aneuploidy risk assessment, dizygotic twin gestations entail doubling the aneuploidy risk; however, the risk for both affected fetuses is theoretical. For monozygotic twin gestations, the aneuploidy risk is congruent, i.e., either both are affected, or both are healthy; thus, the aneuploidy risks for significant gestation based on maternal age can be utilized. When dichorionic diamniotic twin gestation manifests with gender congruence, a distinction between a monozygotic twin gestation with embryonic splitting in the first three days cannot be excluded; however, the vast majority can be assumed to be dizygotic. Noninvasive prenatal testing for twin gestations is available. Serum screening of NT-sequential and QUAD screening are available. If genetic amniocentesis is pursued, the risk of procedure-related loss is in the range of 3%. Additional technical expertise is required to map the twin gestation before the procedure, especially if they are believed to be dizygotic with the same gender. Discordant results with one affected fetus may indicate a selective fetal reduction, and mapping of the twin gestation at the time with genetic amniocentesis assumes additional relevance. [15]

agent1_snippets.title: Personal Health Record -- Definition/Introduction
agent1_snippets.content: Any other information the patient feels is pertinent may also be included. This contrasts with electronic medical records (EMR) and electronic health records (EHR), which are usually maintained by the treating physician or hospital to provide medical care and for billing purposes. A PHR may be either physical or, as has become increasingly common moving forward, electronic. This includes all self-reported and self-recorded health data, including health issues and treatments, records of vital signs and activity recorded with personal devices including smartphones and smartwatches, nutritional data such as diet composition and calorie intake, etc. Several commercial applications are available that allow an individual to maintain a PHR, and some also allow integration of this data with the individual's EMR/EHR, allowing them to take better charge of their own health. The goal of a PHR is to allow the patient to keep their health data on hand for ready access for both themselves and anyone involved in their care while maintaining the privacy and security of this data. A PHR can hence assist in providing tailored medical care. [1]

agent1_snippets.title: Nutrition: Macronutrient Intake, Imbalances, and Interventions -- Definition/Introduction -- Lipids
agent1_snippets.content: Dietary fats can be separated into triglycerides (fats and oils), phospholipids, sterols (cholesterol), and fatty acids. Fatty acids in the diet can be further distinguished according to the presence of double bonds; saturated fats have no double bonds, and unsaturated fats have one or more double bonds. [11] Finally, unsaturated fatty acids can be distinguished by the position of the first double bond counted from the methyl end of the carbon chain into omega-3, omega-6, and omega-9 fatty acids, with the first double bond occurring at the third, sixth, and ninth position, respectively. Different groups of dietary lipids have been known to have distinct physiological properties and health effects. [11]

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 27 -- Level 3 -- Community 2278
agent2_snippets.content: Normal pregnancy and/or delivery is categorized under several multiple‑gestation pregnancy types. It is classified as a twin pregnancy that has resulted in delivery (حمل توأمي، تمت الولادة) as well as a twin pregnancy for which a specific care episode is not defined (حمل توأمي، غير محدد لنوبة الرعاية). It is also classified as a triplet pregnancy that has resulted in delivery (حمل بثلاثة توائم، تمت الولادة). In addition, it is classified as a quadruplet pregnancy for which a specific care episode is not defined (حمل بأربعة توائم، غير محدد لنوبة الرعاية), with duplicate wording for this category resolved as a single classification. Furthermore, Normal pregnancy and/or delivery is linked to the broader concept of multiple pregnancy (حمل متعدد) as its child entity, indicating that it encompasses all forms of pregnancies involving more than one fetus. This hierarchical and categorical organization helps clinicians identify the specific gestational context—twin, triplet, or quadruplet—and whether delivery has occurred or care details remain unspecified, thereby guiding appropriate monitoring and management strategies.

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 3 -- Community 2308
agent2_snippets.content: Normal pregnancy and/or delivery is categorized as several forms of multiple gestation, including twin pregnancy (both delivered and unspecified for a care episode), triplet pregnancy delivered, and quadruplet pregnancy delivered as well as quadruplet pregnancy unspecified for a care episode (noting two similar Arabic expressions for the unspecified category). These specific multiple‑pregnancy classifications are considered instances of “حمل متعدد” (multiple pregnancy), which itself is included in the Standardized MedDRA Query “مضاعفات وعوامل خطر الحمل والمخاض والولادة (باستثناء حالات الإجهاض وولادة جنين ميت) (SMQ)”, a grouping that captures complications and risk factors of pregnancy, labor, and delivery while excluding miscarriage and stillbirth. The SMQ, in turn, is part of the broader category “نواتج متبقية من الحمل” (remaining pregnancy outcomes). Consequently, each listed multiple‑pregnancy type—twin, triplet, and quadruplet pregnancies, whether delivered or unspecified for a care episode—is linked to the same complication‑risk SMQ, situating them within the overall framework of residual pregnancy outcomes and informing clinical assessment and management of normal pregnancy and delivery scenarios that involve multiple fetuses.

All of the listed multiple‑gestation types—twin, triplet, and quadruplet pregnancies—are members of the standardized medical query “مضاعفات وعوامل خطر الحمل والمخاض والولادة (باستثناء حالات الإجهاض وولادة جنين ميت) (SMQ)”, which groups complications and risk factors related to pregnancy, labor, and delivery while excluding miscarriage and stillbirth. In addition, the conditions “تعسر المخاض” (difficult labor) and “رأب عنق الرحم” (cervical insufficiency) are also members of this same SMQ, indicating that they are recognized as important obstetric risk factors. Postpartum obstetric operation is linked to “Zorg en onderzoek onmiddellijk na bevalling” (care and investigation immediately after delivery), reflecting that any obstetric surgical intervention after birth requires prompt postpartum assessment and monitoring. Likewise, the relationship between “Normal pregnancy and/or delivery” and “Zorg en onderzoek onmiddellijk na bevalling” underscores that even uncomplicated deliveries are associated with immediate postpartum care and investigation. Together, these relationships illustrate how various forms of multiple pregnancy, specific obstetric complications, and postpartum procedures are integrated within a unified framework of pregnancy‑related risk assessment and immediate post‑delivery management.
